framework.name: GDPR
framework.description:
REGULATION (EU) 2016/679 OF THE EUROPEAN PARLIAMENT AND OF THE COUNCIL of 27 April 2016 on the protection of natural persons with regard to the processing of personal data and on the free movement of such data, and repealing Directive 95/46/EC (General Data Protection Regulation)
https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/?uri=CELEX:32016R0679

controls.title: Personal data shall be:
controls.description: Personal data shall be:
controls.implementationGuidance: Personal data shall be:

controls.title: processed lawfully, fairly and in a transparent manner in relation to the data subject (‘lawfulness,
controls.description: processed lawfully, fairly and in a transparent manner in relation to the data subject (‘lawfulness, fairness and transparency’);
controls.implementationGuidance: processed lawfully, fairly and in a transparent manner in relation to the data subject (‘lawfulness, fairness and transparency’);

controls.title: collected for specified, explicit and legitimate purposes and not further processed in a manner that
controls.description: collected for specified, explicit and legitimate purposes and not further processed in a manner that is incompatible with those purposes; further processing for archiving purposes in the public interest, scientific or historical research purposes or statistical purposes shall, in accordance with Article 89(1), not be considered to be incompatible with the initial purposes (‘purpose limitation’);
controls.implementationGuidance: collected for specified, explicit and legitimate purposes and not further processed in a manner that is incompatible with those purposes; further processing for archiving purposes in the public interest, scientific or historical research purposes or statistical purposes shall, in accordance with Article 89(1), not be considered to be incompatible with the initial purposes (‘purpose limitation’);

controls.title: adequate, relevant and limited to what is necessary in relation to the purposes for which they are p
controls.description: adequate, relevant and limited to what is necessary in relation to the purposes for which they are processed (‘data minimisation’);
controls.implementationGuidance: adequate, relevant and limited to what is necessary in relation to the purposes for which they are processed (‘data minimisation’);

controls.title: accurate and, where necessary, kept up to date; every reasonable step must be taken to ensure that p
controls.description: accurate and, where necessary, kept up to date; every reasonable step must be taken to ensure that personal data that are inaccurate, having regard to the purposes for which they are processed, are erased or rectified without delay (‘accuracy’);
controls.implementationGuidance: accurate and, where necessary, kept up to date; every reasonable step must be taken to ensure that personal data that are inaccurate, having regard to the purposes for which they are processed, are erased or rectified without delay (‘accuracy’);

controls.title: kept in a form which permits identification of data subjects for no longer than is necessary for the
controls.description: kept in a form which permits identification of data subjects for no longer than is necessary for the purposes for which the personal data are processed; personal data may be stored for longer periods insofar as the personal data will be processed solely for archiving purposes in the public interest, scientific or historical research purposes or statistical purposes in accordance with Article 89(1) subject to implementation of the appropriate technical and organisational measures required by this Regulation in order to safeguard the rights and freedoms of the data subject (‘storage limitation’);
controls.implementationGuidance: kept in a form which permits identification of data subjects for no longer than is necessary for the purposes for which the personal data are processed; personal data may be stored for longer periods insofar as the personal data will be processed solely for archiving purposes in the public interest, scientific or historical research purposes or statistical purposes in accordance with Article 89(1) subject to implementation of the appropriate technical and organisational measures required by this Regulation in order to safeguard the rights and freedoms of the data subject (‘storage limitation’);

controls.title: processed in a manner that ensures appropriate security of the personal data, including protection a
controls.description: processed in a manner that ensures appropriate security of the personal data, including protection against unauthorised or unlawful processing and against accidental loss, destruction or damage, using appropriate technical or organisational measures (‘integrity and confidentiality’).
controls.implementationGuidance: processed in a manner that ensures appropriate security of the personal data, including protection against unauthorised or unlawful processing and against accidental loss, destruction or damage, using appropriate technical or organisational measures (‘integrity and confidentiality’).

controls.title: The controller shall be responsible for, and be able to demonstrate compliance with, paragraph 1 (‘a
controls.description: The controller shall be responsible for, and be able to demonstrate compliance with, paragraph 1 (‘accountability’).
controls.implementationGuidance: The controller shall be responsible for, and be able to demonstrate compliance with, paragraph 1 (‘accountability’).

controls.title: Processing shall be lawful only if and to the extent that at least one of the following applies:
controls.description: Processing shall be lawful only if and to the extent that at least one of the following applies:
controls.implementationGuidance: Processing shall be lawful only if and to the extent that at least one of the following applies:

controls.title: the data subject has given consent to the processing of his or her personal data for one or more spe
controls.description: the data subject has given consent to the processing of his or her personal data for one or more specific purposes;
controls.implementationGuidance: the data subject has given consent to the processing of his or her personal data for one or more specific purposes;

controls.title: processing is necessary for the performance of a contract to which the data subject is party or in o
controls.description: processing is necessary for the performance of a contract to which the data subject is party or in order to take steps at the request of the data subject prior to entering into a contract;
controls.implementationGuidance: processing is necessary for the performance of a contract to which the data subject is party or in order to take steps at the request of the data subject prior to entering into a contract;

controls.title: processing is necessary for compliance with a legal obligation to which the controller is subject;
controls.description: processing is necessary for compliance with a legal obligation to which the controller is subject;
controls.implementationGuidance: processing is necessary for compliance with a legal obligation to which the controller is subject;

controls.title: processing is necessary in order to protect the vital interests of the data subject or of another na
controls.description: processing is necessary in order to protect the vital interests of the data subject or of another natural person;
controls.implementationGuidance: processing is necessary in order to protect the vital interests of the data subject or of another natural person;

controls.title: processing is necessary for the performance of a task carried out in the public interest or in the e
controls.description: processing is necessary for the performance of a task carried out in the public interest or in the exercise of official authority vested in the controller;
controls.implementationGuidance: processing is necessary for the performance of a task carried out in the public interest or in the exercise of official authority vested in the controller;

controls.title: processing is necessary for the purposes of the legitimate interests pursued by the controller or by
controls.description: processing is necessary for the purposes of the legitimate interests pursued by the controller or by a third party, except where such interests are overridden by the interests or fundamental rights and freedoms of the data subject which require protection of personal data, in particular where the data subject is a child.
controls.implementationGuidance: processing is necessary for the purposes of the legitimate interests pursued by the controller or by a third party, except where such interests are overridden by the interests or fundamental rights and freedoms of the data subject which require protection of personal data, in particular where the data subject is a child.

controls.title: Where the processing for a purpose other than that for which the personal data have been collected i
controls.description: Where the processing for a purpose other than that for which the personal data have been collected is not based on the data subject's consent or on a Union or Member State law which constitutes a necessary and proportionate measure in a democratic society to safeguard the objectives referred to in Article 23(1), the controller shall, in order to ascertain whether processing for another purpose is compatible with the purpose for which the personal data are initially collected, take into account, inter alia:
controls.implementationGuidance: Where the processing for a purpose other than that for which the personal data have been collected is not based on the data subject's consent or on a Union or Member State law which constitutes a necessary and proportionate measure in a democratic society to safeguard the objectives referred to in Article 23(1), the controller shall, in order to ascertain whether processing for another purpose is compatible with the purpose for which the personal data are initially collected, take into account, inter alia:

controls.title: any link between the purposes for which the personal data have been collected and the purposes of th
controls.description: any link between the purposes for which the personal data have been collected and the purposes of the intended further processing;
controls.implementationGuidance: any link between the purposes for which the personal data have been collected and the purposes of the intended further processing;

controls.title: the context in which the personal data have been collected, in particular regarding the relationship
controls.description: the context in which the personal data have been collected, in particular regarding the relationship between data subjects and the controller;
controls.implementationGuidance: the context in which the personal data have been collected, in particular regarding the relationship between data subjects and the controller;

controls.title: the nature of the personal data, in particular whether special categories of personal data are proce
controls.description: the nature of the personal data, in particular whether special categories of personal data are processed, pursuant to Article 9, or whether personal data related to criminal convictions and offences are processed, pursuant to Article 10;
controls.implementationGuidance: the nature of the personal data, in particular whether special categories of personal data are processed, pursuant to Article 9, or whether personal data related to criminal convictions and offences are processed, pursuant to Article 10;

controls.title: the possible consequences of the intended further processing for data subjects;
controls.description: the possible consequences of the intended further processing for data subjects;
controls.implementationGuidance: the possible consequences of the intended further processing for data subjects;

controls.title: the existence of appropriate safeguards, which may include encryption or pseudonymisation.
controls.description: the existence of appropriate safeguards, which may include encryption or pseudonymisation.
controls.implementationGuidance: the existence of appropriate safeguards, which may include encryption or pseudonymisation.

controls.title: Where processing is based on consent, the controller shall be able to demonstrate that the data subj
controls.description: Where processing is based on consent, the controller shall be able to demonstrate that the data subject has consented to processing of his or her personal data.
controls.implementationGuidance: Where processing is based on consent, the controller shall be able to demonstrate that the data subject has consented to processing of his or her personal data.

controls.title: If the data subject's consent is given in the context of a written declaration which also concerns o
controls.description: If the data subject's consent is given in the context of a written declaration which also concerns other matters, the request for consent shall be presented in a manner which is clearly distinguishable from the other matters, in an intelligible and easily accessible form, using clear and plain language. Any part of such a declaration which constitutes an infringement of this Regulation shall not be binding.
controls.implementationGuidance: If the data subject's consent is given in the context of a written declaration which also concerns other matters, the request for consent shall be presented in a manner which is clearly distinguishable from the other matters, in an intelligible and easily accessible form, using clear and plain language. Any part of such a declaration which constitutes an infringement of this Regulation shall not be binding.

controls.title: The data subject shall have the right to withdraw his or her consent at any time. The withdrawal of
controls.description: The data subject shall have the right to withdraw his or her consent at any time. The withdrawal of consent shall not affect the lawfulness of processing based on consent before its withdrawal. Prior to giving consent, the data subject shall be informed thereof. It shall be as easy to withdraw as to give consent.
controls.implementationGuidance: The data subject shall have the right to withdraw his or her consent at any time. The withdrawal of consent shall not affect the lawfulness of processing based on consent before its withdrawal. Prior to giving consent, the data subject shall be informed thereof. It shall be as easy to withdraw as to give consent.

controls.title: When assessing whether consent is freely given, utmost account shall be taken of whether, inter alia
controls.description: When assessing whether consent is freely given, utmost account shall be taken of whether, inter alia, the performance of a contract, including the provision of a service, is conditional on consent to the processing of personal data that is not necessary for the performance of that contract.
controls.implementationGuidance: When assessing whether consent is freely given, utmost account shall be taken of whether, inter alia, the performance of a contract, including the provision of a service, is conditional on consent to the processing of personal data that is not necessary for the performance of that contract.

controls.title: Member States may provide by law for a lower age for those purposes provided that such lower age is
controls.description: Member States may provide by law for a lower age for those purposes provided that such lower age is not below 13 years.
controls.implementationGuidance: Member States may provide by law for a lower age for those purposes provided that such lower age is not below 13 years.

controls.title: The controller shall make reasonable efforts to verify in such cases that consent is given or author
controls.description: The controller shall make reasonable efforts to verify in such cases that consent is given or authorised by the holder of parental responsibility over the child, taking into consideration available technology.
controls.implementationGuidance: The controller shall make reasonable efforts to verify in such cases that consent is given or authorised by the holder of parental responsibility over the child, taking into consideration available technology.

controls.title: Processing of personal data revealing racial or ethnic origin, political opinions, religious or phil
controls.description: Processing of personal data revealing racial or ethnic origin, political opinions, religious or philosophical beliefs, or trade union membership, and the processing of genetic data, biometric data for the purpose of uniquely identifying a natural person, data concerning health or data concerning a natural person's sex life or sexual orientation shall be prohibited.
controls.implementationGuidance: Processing of personal data revealing racial or ethnic origin, political opinions, religious or philosophical beliefs, or trade union membership, and the processing of genetic data, biometric data for the purpose of uniquely identifying a natural person, data concerning health or data concerning a natural person's sex life or sexual orientation shall be prohibited.

controls.title: Paragraph 1 shall not apply if one of the following applies:
controls.description: Paragraph 1 shall not apply if one of the following applies:
controls.implementationGuidance: Paragraph 1 shall not apply if one of the following applies:

controls.title: the data subject has given explicit consent to the processing of those personal data for one or more
controls.description: the data subject has given explicit consent to the processing of those personal data for one or more specified purposes, except where Union or Member State law provide that the prohibition referred to in paragraph 1 may not be lifted by the data subject;
controls.implementationGuidance: the data subject has given explicit consent to the processing of those personal data for one or more specified purposes, except where Union or Member State law provide that the prohibition referred to in paragraph 1 may not be lifted by the data subject;

controls.title: processing is necessary for the purposes of carrying out the obligations and exercising specific rig
controls.description: processing is necessary for the purposes of carrying out the obligations and exercising specific rights of the controller or of the data subject in the field of employment and social security and social protection law in so far as it is authorised by Union or Member State law or a collective agreement pursuant to Member State law providing for appropriate safeguards for the fundamental rights and the interests of the data subject;
controls.implementationGuidance: processing is necessary for the purposes of carrying out the obligations and exercising specific rights of the controller or of the data subject in the field of employment and social security and social protection law in so far as it is authorised by Union or Member State law or a collective agreement pursuant to Member State law providing for appropriate safeguards for the fundamental rights and the interests of the data subject;

controls.title: processing is necessary to protect the vital interests of the data subject or of another natural per
controls.description: processing is necessary to protect the vital interests of the data subject or of another natural person where the data subject is physically or legally incapable of giving consent;
controls.implementationGuidance: processing is necessary to protect the vital interests of the data subject or of another natural person where the data subject is physically or legally incapable of giving consent;

controls.title: processing is carried out in the course of its legitimate activities with appropriate safeguards by
controls.description: processing is carried out in the course of its legitimate activities with appropriate safeguards by a foundation, association or any other not-for-profit body with a political, philosophical, religious or trade union aim and on condition that the processing relates solely to the members or to former members of the body or to persons who have regular contact with it in connection with its purposes and that the personal data are not disclosed outside that body without the consent of the data subjects;
controls.implementationGuidance: processing is carried out in the course of its legitimate activities with appropriate safeguards by a foundation, association or any other not-for-profit body with a political, philosophical, religious or trade union aim and on condition that the processing relates solely to the members or to former members of the body or to persons who have regular contact with it in connection with its purposes and that the personal data are not disclosed outside that body without the consent of the data subjects;

controls.title: processing relates to personal data which are manifestly made public by the data subject;
controls.description: processing relates to personal data which are manifestly made public by the data subject;
controls.implementationGuidance: processing relates to personal data which are manifestly made public by the data subject;

controls.title: processing is necessary for the establishment, exercise or defence of legal claims or whenever court
controls.description: processing is necessary for the establishment, exercise or defence of legal claims or whenever courts are acting in their judicial capacity;
controls.implementationGuidance: processing is necessary for the establishment, exercise or defence of legal claims or whenever courts are acting in their judicial capacity;

controls.title: processing is necessary for reasons of substantial public interest, on the basis of Union or Member
controls.description: processing is necessary for reasons of substantial public interest, on the basis of Union or Member State law which shall be proportionate to the aim pursued, respect the essence of the right to data protection and provide for suitable and specific measures to safeguard the fundamental rights and the interests of the data subject;
controls.implementationGuidance: processing is necessary for reasons of substantial public interest, on the basis of Union or Member State law which shall be proportionate to the aim pursued, respect the essence of the right to data protection and provide for suitable and specific measures to safeguard the fundamental rights and the interests of the data subject;

controls.title: processing is necessary for the purposes of preventive or occupational medicine, for the assessment
controls.description: processing is necessary for the purposes of preventive or occupational medicine, for the assessment of the working capacity of the employee, medical diagnosis, the provision of health or social care or treatment or the management of health or social care systems and services on the basis of Union or Member State law or pursuant to contract with a health professional and subject to the conditions and safeguards referred to in paragraph 3;
controls.implementationGuidance: processing is necessary for the purposes of preventive or occupational medicine, for the assessment of the working capacity of the employee, medical diagnosis, the provision of health or social care or treatment or the management of health or social care systems and services on the basis of Union or Member State law or pursuant to contract with a health professional and subject to the conditions and safeguards referred to in paragraph 3;

controls.title: processing is necessary for reasons of public interest in the area of public health, such as protect
controls.description: processing is necessary for reasons of public interest in the area of public health, such as protecting against serious cross-border threats to health or ensuring high standards of quality and safety of health care and of medicinal products or medical devices, on the basis of Union or Member State law which provides for suitable and specific measures to safeguard the rights and freedoms of the data subject, in particular professional secrecy;
controls.implementationGuidance: processing is necessary for reasons of public interest in the area of public health, such as protecting against serious cross-border threats to health or ensuring high standards of quality and safety of health care and of medicinal products or medical devices, on the basis of Union or Member State law which provides for suitable and specific measures to safeguard the rights and freedoms of the data subject, in particular professional secrecy;

controls.title: processing is necessary for archiving purposes in the public interest, scientific or historical rese
controls.description: processing is necessary for archiving purposes in the public interest, scientific or historical research purposes or statistical purposes in accordance with Article 89(1) based on Union or Member State law which shall be proportionate to the aim pursued, respect the essence of the right to data protection and provide for suitable and specific measures to safeguard the fundamental rights and the interests of the data subject.
controls.implementationGuidance: processing is necessary for archiving purposes in the public interest, scientific or historical research purposes or statistical purposes in accordance with Article 89(1) based on Union or Member State law which shall be proportionate to the aim pursued, respect the essence of the right to data protection and provide for suitable and specific measures to safeguard the fundamental rights and the interests of the data subject.

controls.title: Personal data referred to in paragraph 1 may be processed for the purposes referred to in point (h)
controls.description: Personal data referred to in paragraph 1 may be processed for the purposes referred to in point (h) of paragraph 2 when those data are processed by or under the responsibility of a professional subject to the obligation of professional secrecy under Union or Member State law or rules established by national competent bodies or by another person also subject to an obligation of secrecy under Union or Member State law or rules established by national competent bodies.
controls.implementationGuidance: Personal data referred to in paragraph 1 may be processed for the purposes referred to in point (h) of paragraph 2 when those data are processed by or under the responsibility of a professional subject to the obligation of professional secrecy under Union or Member State law or rules established by national competent bodies or by another person also subject to an obligation of secrecy under Union or Member State law or rules established by national competent bodies.

controls.title: If the purposes for which a controller processes personal data do not or do no longer require the id
controls.description: If the purposes for which a controller processes personal data do not or do no longer require the identification of a data subject by the controller, the controller shall not be obliged to maintain, acquire or process additional information in order to identify the data subject for the sole purpose of complying with this Regulation.
controls.implementationGuidance: If the purposes for which a controller processes personal data do not or do no longer require the identification of a data subject by the controller, the controller shall not be obliged to maintain, acquire or process additional information in order to identify the data subject for the sole purpose of complying with this Regulation.

controls.title: Where, in cases referred to in paragraph 1 of this Article, the controller is able to demonstrate th
controls.description: Where, in cases referred to in paragraph 1 of this Article, the controller is able to demonstrate that it is not in a position to identify the data subject, the controller shall inform the data subject accordingly, if possible. In such cases, Articles 15 to 20 shall not apply except where the data subject, for the purpose of exercising his or her rights under those articles, provides additional information enabling his or her identification.
controls.implementationGuidance: Where, in cases referred to in paragraph 1 of this Article, the controller is able to demonstrate that it is not in a position to identify the data subject, the controller shall inform the data subject accordingly, if possible. In such cases, Articles 15 to 20 shall not apply except where the data subject, for the purpose of exercising his or her rights under those articles, provides additional information enabling his or her identification.

controls.title: The controller shall take appropriate measures to provide any information referred to in Articles 13
controls.description: The controller shall take appropriate measures to provide any information referred to in Articles 13 and 14 and any communication under Articles 15 to 22 and 34 relating to processing to the data subject in a concise, transparent, intelligible and easily accessible form, using clear and plain language, in particular for any information addressed specifically to a child. The information shall be provided in writing, or by other means, including, where appropriate, by electronic means. When requested by the data subject, the information may be provided orally, provided that the identity of the data subject is proven by other means.
controls.implementationGuidance: The controller shall take appropriate measures to provide any information referred to in Articles 13 and 14 and any communication under Articles 15 to 22 and 34 relating to processing to the data subject in a concise, transparent, intelligible and easily accessible form, using clear and plain language, in particular for any information addressed specifically to a child. The information shall be provided in writing, or by other means, including, where appropriate, by electronic means. When requested by the data subject, the information may be provided orally, provided that the identity of the data subject is proven by other means.

controls.title: The controller shall facilitate the exercise of data subject rights under Articles 15 to 22. In the
controls.description: The controller shall facilitate the exercise of data subject rights under Articles 15 to 22. In the cases referred to in Article 11(2), the controller shall not refuse to act on the request of the data subject for exercising his or her rights under Articles 15 to 22, unless the controller demonstrates that it is not in a position to identify the data subject.
controls.implementationGuidance: The controller shall facilitate the exercise of data subject rights under Articles 15 to 22. In the cases referred to in Article 11(2), the controller shall not refuse to act on the request of the data subject for exercising his or her rights under Articles 15 to 22, unless the controller demonstrates that it is not in a position to identify the data subject.

controls.title: The controller shall provide information on action taken on a request under Articles 15 to 22 to the
controls.description: The controller shall provide information on action taken on a request under Articles 15 to 22 to the data subject without undue delay and in any event within one month of receipt of the request. That period may be extended by two further months where necessary, taking into account the complexity and number of the requests. The controller shall inform the data subject of any such extension within one month of receipt of the request, together with the reasons for the delay. Where the data subject makes the request by electronic form means, the information shall be provided by electronic means where possible, unless otherwise requested by the data subject.
controls.implementationGuidance: The controller shall provide information on action taken on a request under Articles 15 to 22 to the data subject without undue delay and in any event within one month of receipt of the request. That period may be extended by two further months where necessary, taking into account the complexity and number of the requests. The controller shall inform the data subject of any such extension within one month of receipt of the request, together with the reasons for the delay. Where the data subject makes the request by electronic form means, the information shall be provided by electronic means where possible, unless otherwise requested by the data subject.

controls.title: If the controller does not take action on the request of the data subject, the controller shall info
controls.description: If the controller does not take action on the request of the data subject, the controller shall inform the data subject without delay and at the latest within one month of receipt of the request of the reasons for not taking action and on the possibility of lodging a complaint with a supervisory authority and seeking a judicial remedy.
controls.implementationGuidance: If the controller does not take action on the request of the data subject, the controller shall inform the data subject without delay and at the latest within one month of receipt of the request of the reasons for not taking action and on the possibility of lodging a complaint with a supervisory authority and seeking a judicial remedy.

controls.title: Information provided under Articles 13 and 14 and any communication and any actions taken under Arti
controls.description: Information provided under Articles 13 and 14 and any communication and any actions taken under Articles 15 to 22 and 34 shall be provided free of charge. Where requests from a data subject are manifestly unfounded or excessive, in particular because of their repetitive character, the controller may either:
controls.implementationGuidance: Information provided under Articles 13 and 14 and any communication and any actions taken under Articles 15 to 22 and 34 shall be provided free of charge. Where requests from a data subject are manifestly unfounded or excessive, in particular because of their repetitive character, the controller may either:

controls.title: charge a reasonable fee taking into account the administrative costs of providing the information or
controls.description: charge a reasonable fee taking into account the administrative costs of providing the information or communication or taking the action requested; or
controls.implementationGuidance: charge a reasonable fee taking into account the administrative costs of providing the information or communication or taking the action requested; or

controls.title: refuse to act on the request.
controls.description: refuse to act on the request.
controls.implementationGuidance: refuse to act on the request.

controls.title: The controller shall bear the burden of demonstrating the manifestly unfounded or excessive characte
controls.description: The controller shall bear the burden of demonstrating the manifestly unfounded or excessive character of the request.
controls.implementationGuidance: The controller shall bear the burden of demonstrating the manifestly unfounded or excessive character of the request.

controls.title: Without prejudice to Article 11, where the controller has reasonable doubts concerning the identity
controls.description: Without prejudice to Article 11, where the controller has reasonable doubts concerning the identity of the natural person making the request referred to in Articles 15 to 21, the controller may request the provision of additional information necessary to confirm the identity of the data subject.
controls.implementationGuidance: Without prejudice to Article 11, where the controller has reasonable doubts concerning the identity of the natural person making the request referred to in Articles 15 to 21, the controller may request the provision of additional information necessary to confirm the identity of the data subject.

controls.title: The information to be provided to data subjects pursuant to Articles 13 and 14 may be provided in co
controls.description: The information to be provided to data subjects pursuant to Articles 13 and 14 may be provided in combination with standardised icons in order to give in an easily visible, intelligible and clearly legible manner a meaningful overview of the intended processing. Where the icons are presented electronically they shall be machine-readable.
controls.implementationGuidance: The information to be provided to data subjects pursuant to Articles 13 and 14 may be provided in combination with standardised icons in order to give in an easily visible, intelligible and clearly legible manner a meaningful overview of the intended processing. Where the icons are presented electronically they shall be machine-readable.

controls.title: Where personal data relating to a data subject are collected from the data subject, the controller s
controls.description: Where personal data relating to a data subject are collected from the data subject, the controller shall, at the time when personal data are obtained, provide the data subject with all of the following information:
controls.implementationGuidance: Where personal data relating to a data subject are collected from the data subject, the controller shall, at the time when personal data are obtained, provide the data subject with all of the following information:

controls.title: the identity and the contact details of the controller and, where applicable, of the controller's re
controls.description: the identity and the contact details of the controller and, where applicable, of the controller's representative;
controls.implementationGuidance: the identity and the contact details of the controller and, where applicable, of the controller's representative;

controls.title: the contact details of the data protection officer, where applicable;
controls.description: the contact details of the data protection officer, where applicable;
controls.implementationGuidance: the contact details of the data protection officer, where applicable;

controls.title: the purposes of the processing for which the personal data are intended as well as the legal basis f
controls.description: the purposes of the processing for which the personal data are intended as well as the legal basis for the processing;
controls.implementationGuidance: the purposes of the processing for which the personal data are intended as well as the legal basis for the processing;

controls.title: where the processing is based on point (f) of Article 6(1), the legitimate interests pursued by the
controls.description: where the processing is based on point (f) of Article 6(1), the legitimate interests pursued by the controller or by a third party;
controls.implementationGuidance: where the processing is based on point (f) of Article 6(1), the legitimate interests pursued by the controller or by a third party;

controls.title: the recipients or categories of recipients of the personal data, if any;
controls.description: the recipients or categories of recipients of the personal data, if any;
controls.implementationGuidance: the recipients or categories of recipients of the personal data, if any;

controls.title: where applicable, the fact that the controller intends to transfer personal data to a third country
controls.description: where applicable, the fact that the controller intends to transfer personal data to a third country or international organisation and the existence or absence of an adequacy decision by the Commission, or in the case of transfers referred to in Article 46 or 47, or the second subparagraph of Article 49(1), reference to the appropriate or suitable safeguards and the means by which to obtain a copy of them or where they have been made available.
controls.implementationGuidance: where applicable, the fact that the controller intends to transfer personal data to a third country or international organisation and the existence or absence of an adequacy decision by the Commission, or in the case of transfers referred to in Article 46 or 47, or the second subparagraph of Article 49(1), reference to the appropriate or suitable safeguards and the means by which to obtain a copy of them or where they have been made available.

controls.title: In addition to the information referred to in paragraph 1, the controller shall, at the time when pe
controls.description: In addition to the information referred to in paragraph 1, the controller shall, at the time when personal data are obtained, provide the data subject with the following further information necessary to ensure fair and transparent processing:
controls.implementationGuidance: In addition to the information referred to in paragraph 1, the controller shall, at the time when personal data are obtained, provide the data subject with the following further information necessary to ensure fair and transparent processing:

controls.title: the period for which the personal data will be stored, or if that is not possible, the criteria used
controls.description: the period for which the personal data will be stored, or if that is not possible, the criteria used to determine that period;
controls.implementationGuidance: the period for which the personal data will be stored, or if that is not possible, the criteria used to determine that period;

controls.title: the existence of the right to request from the controller access to and rectification or erasure of
controls.description: the existence of the right to request from the controller access to and rectification or erasure of personal data or restriction of processing concerning the data subject or to object to processing as well as the right to data portability;

controls.description: Taking into account the state of the art, the cost of implementation and the nature, scope, context and purposes of processing as well as the risks of varying likelihood and severity for rights and freedoms of natural persons posed by the processing, the controller shall, both at the time of the determination of the means for processing and at the time of the processing itself, implement appropriate technical and organisational measures, such as pseudonymisation, which are designed to implement data-protection principles, such as data minimisation, in an effective manner and to integrate the necessary safeguards into the processing in order to meet the requirements of this Regulation and protect the rights of data subjects.
controls.implementationGuidance: Taking into account the state of the art, the cost of implementation and the nature, scope, context and purposes of processing as well as the risks of varying likelihood and severity for rights and freedoms of natural persons posed by the processing, the controller shall, both at the time of the determination of the means for processing and at the time of the processing itself, implement appropriate technical and organisational measures, such as pseudonymisation, which are designed to implement data-protection principles, such as data minimisation, in an effective manner and to integrate the necessary safeguards into the processing in order to meet the requirements of this Regulation and protect the rights of data subjects.

controls.title: The controller shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures for ensuring that,
controls.description: The controller shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures for ensuring that, by default, only personal data which are necessary for each specific purpose of the processing are processed. That obligation applies to the amount of personal data collected, the extent of their processing, the period of their storage and their accessibility. In particular, such measures shall ensure that by default personal data are not made accessible without the individual's intervention to an indefinite number of natural persons.
controls.implementationGuidance: The controller shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures for ensuring that, by default, only personal data which are necessary for each specific purpose of the processing are processed. That obligation applies to the amount of personal data collected, the extent of their processing, the period of their storage and their accessibility. In particular, such measures shall ensure that by default personal data are not made accessible without the individual's intervention to an indefinite number of natural persons.

controls.title: Where two or more controllers jointly determine the purposes and means of processing, they shall be
controls.description: Where two or more controllers jointly determine the purposes and means of processing, they shall be joint controllers. They shall in a transparent manner determine their respective responsibilities for compliance with the obligations under this Regulation, in particular as regards the exercising of the rights of the data subject and their respective duties to provide the information referred to in Articles 13 and 14, by means of an arrangement between them unless, and in so far as, the respective responsibilities of the controllers are determined by Union or Member State law to which the controllers are subject. The arrangement may designate a contact point for data subjects.
controls.implementationGuidance: Where two or more controllers jointly determine the purposes and means of processing, they shall be joint controllers. They shall in a transparent manner determine their respective responsibilities for compliance with the obligations under this Regulation, in particular as regards the exercising of the rights of the data subject and their respective duties to provide the information referred to in Articles 13 and 14, by means of an arrangement between them unless, and in so far as, the respective responsibilities of the controllers are determined by Union or Member State law to which the controllers are subject. The arrangement may designate a contact point for data subjects.

controls.description: When the personal data breach is likely to result in a high risk to the rights and freedoms of natural persons, the controller shall communicate the personal data breach to the data subject without undue delay.
controls.implementationGuidance: When the personal data breach is likely to result in a high risk to the rights and freedoms of natural persons, the controller shall communicate the personal data breach to the data subject without undue delay.

controls.title: The communication to the data subject referred to in paragraph 1 of this Article shall describe in c
controls.description: The communication to the data subject referred to in paragraph 1 of this Article shall describe in clear and plain language the nature of the personal data breach and contain at least the information and measures referred to in points (b), (c) and (d) of Article 33(3).
controls.implementationGuidance: The communication to the data subject referred to in paragraph 1 of this Article shall describe in clear and plain language the nature of the personal data breach and contain at least the information and measures referred to in points (b), (c) and (d) of Article 33(3).

controls.title: The communication to the data subject referred to in paragraph 1 shall not be required if any of the
controls.description: The communication to the data subject referred to in paragraph 1 shall not be required if any of the following conditions are met:
controls.implementationGuidance: The communication to the data subject referred to in paragraph 1 shall not be required if any of the following conditions are met:

controls.title: the controller has implemented appropriate technical and organisational protection measures, and tho
controls.description: the controller has implemented appropriate technical and organisational protection measures, and those measures were applied to the personal data affected by the personal data breach, in particular those that render the personal data unintelligible to any person who is not authorised to access it, such as encryption;
controls.implementationGuidance: the controller has implemented appropriate technical and organisational protection measures, and those measures were applied to the personal data affected by the personal data breach, in particular those that render the personal data unintelligible to any person who is not authorised to access it, such as encryption;

controls.title: the controller has taken subsequent measures which ensure that the high risk to the rights and freed
controls.description: the controller has taken subsequent measures which ensure that the high risk to the rights and freedoms of data subjects referred to in paragraph 1 is no longer likely to materialise;
controls.implementationGuidance: the controller has taken subsequent measures which ensure that the high risk to the rights and freedoms of data subjects referred to in paragraph 1 is no longer likely to materialise;

controls.title: it would involve disproportionate effort. In such a case, there shall instead be a public communicat
controls.description: it would involve disproportionate effort. In such a case, there shall instead be a public communication or similar measure whereby the data subjects are informed in an equally effective manner.
controls.implementationGuidance: it would involve disproportionate effort. In such a case, there shall instead be a public communication or similar measure whereby the data subjects are informed in an equally effective manner.

controls.title: If the controller has not already communicated the personal data breach to the data subject, the sup
controls.description: If the controller has not already communicated the personal data breach to the data subject, the supervisory authority, having considered the likelihood of the personal data breach resulting in a high risk, may require it to do so or may decide that any of the conditions referred to in paragraph 3 are met.
controls.implementationGuidance: If the controller has not already communicated the personal data breach to the data subject, the supervisory authority, having considered the likelihood of the personal data breach resulting in a high risk, may require it to do so or may decide that any of the conditions referred to in paragraph 3 are met.

controls.title: Where a type of processing in particular using new technologies, and taking into account the nature,
controls.description: Where a type of processing in particular using new technologies, and taking into account the nature, scope, context and purposes of the processing, is likely to result in a high risk to the rights and freedoms of natural persons, the controller shall, prior to the processing, carry out an assessment of the impact of the envisaged processing operations on the protection of personal data. A single assessment may address a set of similar processing operations that present similar high risks.
controls.implementationGuidance: Where a type of processing in particular using new technologies, and taking into account the nature, scope, context and purposes of the processing, is likely to result in a high risk to the rights and freedoms of natural persons, the controller shall, prior to the processing, carry out an assessment of the impact of the envisaged processing operations on the protection of personal data. A single assessment may address a set of similar processing operations that present similar high risks.

controls.description: The controller shall seek the advice of the data protection officer, where designated, when carrying out a data protection impact assessment.

controls.implementationGuidance: the measures envisaged to address the risks, including safeguards, security measures and mechanisms to ensure the protection of personal data and to demonstrate compliance with this Regulation taking into account the rights and legitimate interests of data subjects and other persons concerned.

controls.title: Compliance with approved codes of conduct referred to in Article 40 by the relevant controllers or p
controls.description: Compliance with approved codes of conduct referred to in Article 40 by the relevant controllers or processors shall be taken into due account in assessing the impact of the processing operations performed by such controllers or processors, in particular for the purposes of a data protection impact assessment.
controls.implementationGuidance: Compliance with approved codes of conduct referred to in Article 40 by the relevant controllers or processors shall be taken into due account in assessing the impact of the processing operations performed by such controllers or processors, in particular for the purposes of a data protection impact assessment.

controls.title: Where appropriate, the controller shall seek the views of data subjects or their representatives on
controls.description: Where appropriate, the controller shall seek the views of data subjects or their representatives on the intended processing, without prejudice to the protection of commercial or public interests or the security of processing operations.
controls.implementationGuidance: Where appropriate, the controller shall seek the views of data subjects or their representatives on the intended processing, without prejudice to the protection of commercial or public interests or the security of processing operations.

controls.title: Where processing pursuant to point (c) or (e) of Article 6(1) has a legal basis in Union law or in t
controls.description: Where processing pursuant to point (c) or (e) of Article 6(1) has a legal basis in Union law or in the law of the Member State to which the controller is subject, that law regulates the specific processing operation or set of operations in question, and a data protection impact assessment has already been carried out as part of a general impact assessment in the context of the adoption of that legal basis, paragraphs 1 to 7 shall not apply unless Member States deem it to be necessary to carry out such an assessment prior to processing activities.

controls.title: Where necessary, the controller shall carry out a review to assess if processing is performed in acc

controls.description: The controller shall consult the supervisory authority prior to processing where a data protection impact assessment under Article 35 indicates that the processing would result in a high risk in the absence of measures taken by the controller to mitigate the risk.
controls.implementationGuidance: The controller shall consult the supervisory authority prior to processing where a data protection impact assessment under Article 35 indicates that the processing would result in a high risk in the absence of measures taken by the controller to mitigate the risk.

controls.title: A group of undertakings may appoint a single data protection officer provided that a data protection
controls.description: A group of undertakings may appoint a single data protection officer provided that a data protection officer is easily accessible from each establishment.
controls.implementationGuidance: A group of undertakings may appoint a single data protection officer provided that a data protection officer is easily accessible from each establishment.

controls.title: Where the controller or the processor is a public authority or body, a single data protection office
controls.description: Where the controller or the processor is a public authority or body, a single data protection officer may be designated for several such authorities or bodies, taking account of their organisational structure and size.
controls.implementationGuidance: Where the controller or the processor is a public authority or body, a single data protection officer may be designated for several such authorities or bodies, taking account of their organisational structure and size.

controls.title: In cases other than those referred to in paragraph 1, the controller or processor or associations an
controls.description: In cases other than those referred to in paragraph 1, the controller or processor or associations and other bodies representing categories of controllers or processors may or, where required by Union or Member State law shall, designate a data protection officer. The data protection officer may act for such associations and other bodies representing controllers or processors.

controls.title: The data protection officer shall be designated on the basis of professional qualities and, in parti
controls.description: The data protection officer shall be designated on the basis of professional qualities and, in particular, expert knowledge of data protection law and practices and the ability to fulfil the tasks referred to in Article 39.
controls.implementationGuidance: The data protection officer shall be designated on the basis of professional qualities and, in particular, expert knowledge of data protection law and practices and the ability to fulfil the tasks referred to in Article 39.

controls.description: The controller or the processor shall publish the contact details of the data protection officer and communicate them to the supervisory authority.

controls.title: The controller and the processor shall ensure that the data protection officer is involved, properly
controls.description: The controller and the processor shall ensure that the data protection officer is involved, properly and in a timely manner, in all issues which relate to the protection of personal data.
controls.implementationGuidance: The controller and the processor shall ensure that the data protection officer is involved, properly and in a timely manner, in all issues which relate to the protection of personal data.

controls.title: The controller and processor shall support the data protection officer in performing the tasks refer
controls.description: The controller and processor shall support the data protection officer in performing the tasks referred to in Article 39 by providing resources necessary to carry out those tasks and access to personal data and processing operations, and to maintain his or her expert knowledge.

controls.title: The controller and processor shall ensure that the data protection officer does not receive any inst
controls.description: The controller and processor shall ensure that the data protection officer does not receive any instructions regarding the exercise of those tasks. He or she shall not be dismissed or penalised by the controller or the processor for performing his tasks. The data protection officer shall directly report to the highest management level of the controller or the processor.

controls.title: In addition to adherence by controllers or processors subject to this Regulation, codes of conduct a
controls.description: In addition to adherence by controllers or processors subject to this Regulation, codes of conduct approved pursuant to paragraph 5 of this Article and having general validity pursuant to paragraph 9 of this Article may also be adhered to by controllers or processors that are not subject to this Regulation pursuant to Article 3 in order to provide appropriate safeguards within the framework of personal data transfers to third countries or international organisations under the terms referred to in point (e) of Article 46(2). Such controllers or processors shall make binding and enforceable commitments, via contractual or other legally binding instruments, to apply those appropriate safeguards including with regard to the rights of data subjects.
controls.implementationGuidance: In addition to adherence by controllers or processors subject to this Regulation, codes of conduct approved pursuant to paragraph 5 of this Article and having general validity pursuant to paragraph 9 of this Article may also be adhered to by controllers or processors that are not subject to this Regulation pursuant to Article 3 in order to provide appropriate safeguards within the framework of personal data transfers to third countries or international organisations under the terms referred to in point (e) of Article 46(2). controls.title: Associations and other bodies referred to in paragraph 2 of this Article which intend to prepare a c
controls.description: Associations and other bodies referred to in paragraph 2 of this Article which intend to prepare a code of conduct or to amend or extend an existing code shall submit the draft code, amendment or extension to the supervisory authority which is competent pursuant to Article 55. The supervisory authority shall provide an opinion on whether the draft code, amendment or extension complies with this Regulation and shall approve that draft code, amendment or extension if it finds that it provides sufficient appropriate safeguards.
controls.implementationGuidance: Associations and other bodies referred to in paragraph 2 of this Article which intend to prepare a code of conduct or to amend or extend an existing code shall submit the draft code, amendment or extension to the supervisory authority which is competent pursuant to Article 55. controls.description: A certification pursuant to this Article does not reduce the responsibility of the controller or the processor for compliance with this Regulation and is without prejudice to the tasks and powers of the supervisory authorities which are competent pursuant to Article 55 or 56.

controls.description: Certification shall be issued to a controller or processor for a maximum period of three years and may be renewed, under the same conditions, provided that the relevant criteria continue to be met. controls.title: Any transfer of personal data which are undergoing processing or are intended for processing after t
controls.description: Any transfer of personal data which are undergoing processing or are intended for processing after transfer to a third country or to an international organisation shall take place only if, subject to the other provisions of this Regulation, the conditions laid down in this Chapter are complied with by the controller and processor, including for onward transfers of personal data from the third country or an international organisation to another third country or to another international organisation. All provisions in this Chapter shall be applied in order to ensure that the level of protection of natural persons guaranteed by this Regulation is not undermined.
controls.implementationGuidance: Any transfer of personal data which are undergoing processing or are intended for processing after transfer to a third country or to an international organisation shall take place only if, subject to the other provisions of this Regulation, the conditions laid down in this Chapter are complied with by the controller and processor, including for onward transfers of personal data from the third country or an international organisation to another third country or to another international organisation. All provisions in this Chapter shall be applied in order to ensure that the level of protection of natural persons guaranteed by this Regulation is not undermined.

controls.title: A transfer of personal data to a third country or an international organisation may take place where
controls.description: A transfer of personal data to a third country or an international organisation may take place where the Commission has decided that the third country, a territory or one or more specified sectors within that third country, or the international organisation in question ensures an adequate level of protection. Such a transfer shall not require any specific authorisation.
controls.implementationGuidance: A transfer of personal data to a third country or an international organisation may take place where the Commission has decided that the third country, a territory or one or more specified sectors within that third country, or the international organisation in question ensures an adequate level of protection. controls.description: In the absence of a decision pursuant to Article 45(3), a controller or processor may transfer personal data to a third country or an international organisation only if the controller or processor has provided appropriate safeguards, and on condition that enforceable data subject rights and effective legal remedies for data subjects are available.
controls.implementationGuidance: In the absence of a decision pursuant to Article 45(3), a controller or processor may transfer personal data to a third country or an international organisation only if the controller or processor has provided appropriate safeguards, and on condition that enforceable data subject rights and effective legal remedies for data subjects are available.

controls.title: Any controller involved in processing shall be liable for the damage caused by processing which infr
controls.description: Any controller involved in processing shall be liable for the damage caused by processing which infringes this Regulation. A processor shall be liable for the damage caused by processing only where it has not complied with obligations of this Regulation specifically directed to processors or where it has acted outside or contrary to lawful instructions of the controller.

controls.title: A controller or processor shall be exempt from liability under paragraph 2 if it proves that it is n
controls.description: A controller or processor shall be exempt from liability under paragraph 2 if it proves that it is not in any way responsible for the event giving rise to the damage.

controls.title: Where more than one controller or processor, or both a controller and a processor, are involved in t
controls.description: Where more than one controller or processor, or both a controller and a processor, are involved in the same processing and where they are, under paragraphs 2 and 3, responsible for any damage caused by processing, each controller or processor shall be held liable for the entire damage in order to ensure effective compensation of the data subject.

controls.title: Where a controller or processor has, in accordance with paragraph 4, paid full compensation for the
controls.description: Where a controller or processor has, in accordance with paragraph 4, paid full compensation for the damage suffered, that controller or processor shall be entitled to claim back from the other controllers or processors involved in the same processing that part of the compensation corresponding to their part of responsibility for the damage, in accordance with the conditions set out in paragraph 2.

controls.title: Member States shall lay down the rules on other penalties applicable to infringements of this Regula
controls.description: Member States shall lay down the rules on other penalties applicable to infringements of this Regulation in particular for infringements which are not subject to administrative fines pursuant to Article 83, and shall take all measures necessary to ensure that they are implemented. Such penalties shall be effective, proportionate and dissuasive.

Such penalties shall be effective, proportionate and dissuasive.

controls.title: For processing carried out for journalistic purposes or the purpose of academic artistic or literary
controls.description: For processing carried out for journalistic purposes or the purpose of academic artistic or literary expression, Member States shall provide for exemptions or derogations from Chapter II (principles), Chapter III (rights of the data subject), Chapter IV (controller and processor), Chapter V (transfer of personal data to third countries or international organisations), Chapter VI (independent supervisory authorities), Chapter VII (cooperation and consistency) and Chapter IX (specific data processing situations) if they are necessary to reconcile the right to the protection of personal data with the freedom of expression and information.

controls.title: Member States may further determine the specific conditions for the processing of a national identif
controls.description: Member States may further determine the specific conditions for the processing of a national identification number or any other identifier of general application. In that case the national identification number or any other identifier of general application shall be used only under appropriate safeguards for the rights and freedoms of the data subject pursuant to this Regulation.

controls.title: Member States may, by law or by collective agreements, provide for more specific rules to ensure the
controls.description: Member States may, by law or by collective agreements, provide for more specific rules to ensure the protection of the rights and freedoms in respect of the processing of employees' personal data in the employment context, in particular for the purposes of the recruitment, the performance of the contract of employment, including discharge of obligations laid down by law or by collective agreements, management, planning and organisation of work, equality and diversity in the workplace, health and safety at work, protection of employer's or customer's property and for the purposes of the exercise and enjoyment, on an individual or collective basis, of rights and benefits related to employment, and for the purpose of the termination of the employment relationship.

controls.title: Processing for archiving purposes in the public interest, scientific or historical research purposes
controls.description: Processing for archiving purposes in the public interest, scientific or historical research purposes or statistical purposes, shall be subject to appropriate safeguards, in accordance with this Regulation, for the rights and freedoms of the data subject. Those safeguards shall ensure that technical and organisational measures are in place in particular in order to ensure respect for the principle of data minimisation. Those measures may include pseudonymisation provided that those purposes can be fulfilled in that manner. Where those purposes can be fulfilled by further processing which does not permit or no longer permits the identification of data subjects, those purposes shall be fulfilled in that manner.
controls.implementationGuidance: Processing for archiving purposes in the public interest, scientific or historical research purposes or statistical purposes, shall be subject to appropriate safeguards, in accordance with this Regulation, for the rights and freedoms of the data subject. Those measures may include pseudonymisation provided that those purposes can be fulfilled in that manner. Where those purposes can be fulfilled by further processing which does not permit or no longer permits the identification of data subjects, those purposes shall be fulfilled in that manner.

controls.title: Churches and religious associations which apply comprehensive rules in accordance with paragraph 1 o
controls.description: Churches and religious associations which apply comprehensive rules in accordance with paragraph 1 of this Article shall be subject to the supervision of an independent supervisory authority, which may be specific, provided that it fulfils the conditions laid down in Chapter VI of this Regulation.
